data.title: The White House plans to convene a meeting with banking and crypto industry executives next Monday to push forward with the Clarity Act.
data.description: The White House plans to convene a meeting next Monday with executives from the banking and cryptocurrency industries in an attempt to find a path forward for the landmark CLARITY Act, which is currently stalled due to differing stances between the two sectors. The meeting, reportedly chaired by the White House Crypto Council, will include executives from several industry associations, and will focus on how the bill regulates interest and other revenue arrangements generated by cryptocurrency companies holding dollar-pegged stablecoins for their clients. Analysts believe the meeting could help ease the differences between the banking and cryptocurrency industries on relevant provisions and reflects the Trump administration's desire to expedite the bill's progress. (Reuters)

data.title: Sam Altman-Founded Projects May Collaborate on Human-Only Social Media Platform
data.description:
On January 29, reports indicate that two projects co-founded by Sam Altman might join forces to create a social media platform exclusively for humans. According to BlockBeats, anonymous sources suggest that OpenAI, an artificial intelligence company co-founded by Altman, may utilize the eye-scanning device Orb from the Worldcoin project to verify users on the planned social network. Worldcoin, developed by Tools for Humanity, another venture co-founded by Altman, has previously attracted millions of users to register and receive its cryptocurrency WLD through a 'scan-to-earn' model.

The report adds that a small team at OpenAI is also considering using Apple's Face ID for biometric verification to confirm user identities. Analysts speculate that OpenAI's strategy might leverage the success of ChatGPT and Sora to establish a social media platform free from bots, distinguishing it from current networks like X (formerly Twitter), Instagram, and TikTok, which are plagued by bot issues. In April, tech media outlet The Verge reported that OpenAI was developing social network products.

data.title: CT3 Introduces Decentralized Storage Solution with Enhanced Features
data.description: CT3 has unveiled CT3 Secure Storage, a decentralized storage platform designed to provide Web 3.0 data ownership with the ease of cloud services. According to NS3.AI, the platform has transitioned from Solana to Polygon to improve reliability and scalability. It incorporates NFT keys for secure file access and offers a transparent pay-as-you-go pricing model. CT3 is also planning to introduce DApp hosting capabilities and is targeting large-scale corporate storage contracts.

data.title: MegaETH: Public mainnet will launch on February 9th.
data.description: MegaETH officially announced on the X platform that its public mainnet will be launched on February 9, 2026.

data.title: MegaETH's native stablecoin, MegaUSD, has been listed on DefiLlama, with a current market capitalization of $6.1 million.
data.description: DefiLlama officially announced on its X platform that MegaETH's native stablecoin, MegaUSD (MUSD), is now listed on the platform's stablecoin tracking dashboard. Data shows that MegaUSD (MUSD) currently has a market capitalization of $6.1 million.

data.title: Fed's Recent Statement Reflects Hawkish Stance, Says MAI Capital Strategist
data.description: The Federal Reserve's latest statement and press conference have been notably hawkish, according to PANews. Chris Grisanti, Chief Market Strategist at MAI Capital Management, highlighted that the description of economic activity has been upgraded from 'moderate' to 'robust,' and references to employment downside risks have been removed. During the press conference, Fed Chair Jerome Powell noted that after a period of weakness last year, the employment situation has 'stabilized.' While inflation is showing signs of stability, it remains 'slightly elevated.' Overall, the Fed's focus has shifted from unemployment to inflation. Grisanti believes that interest rates are unlikely to be cut in the short term. Furthermore, given the strong market performance and ongoing economic strength, he suggests that rate cuts may not occur in 2026, a stance that is more hawkish than current market expectations.

data.title: Optimism Aligns OP Token with Superchain's Economic Performance
data.description:
On January 29, Optimism's governance approved a proposal to closely link the OP token with the economic performance of the Superchain. According to BlockBeats, this marks a significant shift in how one of Ethereum's largest Layer 2 ecosystems manages token value and revenue.

The proposal outlines that the Optimism Foundation will allocate 50% of the Superchain sequencer's net income to regularly repurchase OP tokens during a 12-month pilot starting in February. The remaining income will continue to support the ecosystem fund, project grants, and operations.

Data from the Optimism on-chain governance portal shows that the proposal passed with 84.4% support after several days of discussion among representatives and token holders.

This initiative is Optimism's first formal attempt to tie the demand for OP tokens to the activity across the Superchain network. The Superchain is an expanding collection of chains built on the OP Stack, including the OP Mainnet, Base, Unichain, World Chain, Soneium, and Ink.

As of the time of reporting, the market capitalization of OP stands at $583 million, down 87.6% from its all-time high, with the token price at $0.3, a 93.8% decrease from its peak.

data.title: Gold's Rally Matches Bitcoin's Market Cap Amid Midterm Year Trends
data.description: Benjamin Cowen, founder and CEO of Into The Cryptoverse, posted on X that the recent rally in gold equaled the entire market capitalization of Bitcoin. He emphasized the importance of respecting market cycles, noting that midterm election years tend to be challenging for Bitcoin. Cowen suggested that historically, it is often advisable to overlook Bitcoin until at least the summer of a midterm year.

data.title: Potential Risks in the Market Highlighted by Expert
data.description: Matt Hougan, chief investment officer at Bitwise Asset Management, posted on X, expressing concerns about potential risks in the market. He emphasized that these risks could persist for some time, indicating a cautious outlook for investors. Hougan's comments reflect a broader sentiment of uncertainty in the financial landscape, urging stakeholders to remain vigilant.

data.title: Microsoft Stock Declines Over 7% Following Q4 2025 Earnings Report
data.description:
Microsoft's stock, trading under the symbol $MSFT, experienced a significant decline of over 7% following the release of its Q4 2025 earnings report. The Kobeissi Letter posted on X that the drop was primarily attributed to a slowdown in the company's cloud growth, which has been a key driver of its revenue in recent years.

The earnings report revealed that while Microsoft continues to see growth in its cloud services, the pace has decelerated compared to previous quarters. This slowdown has raised concerns among investors, leading to the sharp decline in stock value.

Microsoft's cloud segment, which includes Azure and other cloud services, has been a major contributor to the company's overall financial performance. However, the latest figures suggest that the rapid expansion seen in earlier periods is beginning to taper off.

Analysts are closely monitoring the situation to determine whether this trend will continue and how it might impact Microsoft's future growth prospects. The company's ability to innovate and adapt in the competitive cloud market will be crucial in maintaining investor confidence and sustaining its market position.

data.title: Benefits of Tokenized Stocks Highlighted by Investment Expert
data.description: Matt Hougan, chief investment officer at Bitwise Asset Management, posted on X about the advantages of tokenized stocks, comparing them to exchange-traded funds (ETFs). He emphasized several benefits, including 24/7 trading, global distribution, and T+0 settlement, which allows for immediate transaction completion. Hougan noted that tokenized stocks, similar to ETFs, offer diverse advantages that cater to different users' needs. While some investors may prioritize certain benefits, such as tax efficiency, others may find value in different aspects of these financial instruments. Hougan highlighted the versatility of tokenized stocks, suggesting they can serve various purposes for different individuals, much like ETFs do.

data.title: South Korea's People Power Party Challenges Proposed Crypto Exchange Stake Limits
data.description: South Korea's People Power Party (PPP) has expressed opposition to the Financial Services Commission's (FSC) proposal to restrict major shareholders' stakes in cryptocurrency exchanges. According to NS3.AI, the PPP contends that these stake limitations may lead to management confusion and potential capital flight. The party also questions the timing and underlying motives of the proposed regulation, suggesting it could negatively impact the crypto industry in South Korea.

data.title: Market Sentiment Shifts as Risk-Off Season Begins
data.description: Crypto KOL Ted posted on X, indicating a shift in market sentiment as the risk-off season appears to be starting. This change suggests investors are becoming more cautious, potentially moving away from high-risk assets. The sentiment reflects broader market trends where uncertainty or volatility prompts a more conservative investment approach.

data.title: Coinbase adds Hyperliquid (HYPE) to its asset roadmap
data.description: Coinbase Markets announced on its X platform that Hyperliquid (HYPE) has been added to its asset roadmap. Asset roadmaps are typically used to disclose tokens that a platform is evaluating or planning to support, but they do not constitute a final listing commitment. Specific listing dates and trading support details are subject to official announcements.

data.title: Spot Gold Experiences Rapid Pullback After Approaching $5,600
data.description: Spot gold prices experienced a swift decline after nearing the $5,600 mark. According to PANews, the current price is reported at $5,530 per ounce, with the daily increase narrowing to 2%.

data.title: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has issued new guidance on tokenized securities, clarifying that tokenization does not alter the application of securities laws.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has issued new guidance on tokenized securities, clarifying that whether a security is regulated depends on its legal nature, not on whether it is tokenized. Tokenization itself does not change the scope of securities laws. The statement further distinguishes between issuer-sponsored tokenized securities and third-party tokenized securities, and covers different structures such as custody and synthetic models, aiming to provide clearer regulatory guidance for market participants to conduct related business within a compliant framework.

data.title: Spot gold approaches $5,600
data.description: Spot gold rose more than $170 in early trading, reaching $5,595 per ounce, a 3% increase on the day. (Jinshi)
